name: Almadén Mercury and Castles Route
name_original: Capilla desde Chillón – circular por Castillo de Chillón y las minas de Almadén
description: This difficult 50.3-mile road cycling route near Chillón explores historic castles, mining landscapes, and offers panoramic views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Chillón features a landscape characterized by rolling hills and historical mining areas, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region presents a mix of challenging ascents and descents, with routes often traversing rural landscapes. Cyclists can expect to encounter a blend of open countryside and areas with historical significance. The elevation changes provide opportunities for demanding rides. Best road cycling routes around Chillón The most popular road cycling route is Almadén Mercury and Castles Route, a 50.3 miles (80.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route explores historical sites and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Almadén Circular via Saceruela and Puebla de, a difficult 87.6 miles (141.0 km) path. This route features challenging climbs and expansive views of the surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Miner's Monument – Almadén Bullring loop from Almadén, a 51.9 miles (83.5 km) trail leading through historical mining landscapes and local landmarks, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Chillón is defined by rolling hills, historical mining areas, and rural landscapes. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Chillón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Chillón?
A: There are currently over 5 road cycling routes available around Chillón on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Chillón?
A: Road cycling in Chillón is characterized by rolling hills and historical mining areas. You'll encounter varied terrain with a mix of challenging ascents and descents, often traversing rural landscapes and open countryside.

Q: Are there challenging road bike climbs in Chillón?
A: Yes, Chillón offers several challenging road bike climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067787643/almaden-circular-via-saceruela-and-puebla-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Circular via Saceruela and Puebla de&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation changes, providing demanding rides with expansive views.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes around Chillón?
A: Absolutely. Chillón is home to several long-distance routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067787643/almaden-circular-via-saceruela-and-puebla-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Circular via Saceruela and Puebla de&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 87.6-mile (141.0 km) path, ideal for those seeking a longer ride.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Chillón?
A: Yes, many routes in Chillón are designed as loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miner's Monument – Almadén Bullring loop from Almadén&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 51.9-mile (83.5 km) circular trail that takes you through historical mining landscapes.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I explore while road cycling in Chillón?
A: Chillón's routes often pass by significant historical sites. You can explore places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Mining Park&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Bullring&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Museum&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085208308/almaden-mercury-and-castles-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Mercury and Castles Route&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights historical sites and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any moderate or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Chillón?
A: While many routes are difficult, Chillón does offer moderate options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miner's Monument – Almadén Bullring loop from Guadalmez&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 49.4-mile (79.5 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Bullring – Almadén Mining Park loop from Almadén&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, moderate 13.8-mile (22.2 km) option.

Q: What is the best time for road biking in Chillón?
A: The best time for road biking in Chillón generally aligns with spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite hot, making early mornings or late afternoons more suitable for rides. Winters are typically mild but can be damp.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Chillón?
A: Many routes around Chillón, particularly those starting from towns like Almadén, offer designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road bike trails in Chillón?
A: Routes often pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find local cafes and shops for refreshments. Planning your route with komoot allows you to identify potential stops along the way.

Q: What should I wear for road biking in Chillón?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for elevation changes, layered clothing is recommended. Performance cycling gear suitable for changing weather conditions, including a windbreaker or light jacket, is advisable. Don't forget sun protection, especially during warmer months.

Q: How can I check the weather forecast for road biking in Chillón?
A: Before heading out, always check a reliable local weather forecast for Chillón. This will help you prepare for temperature changes, potential rain, or strong winds, ensuring a safer and more comfortable ride.
tips: Chillón is a small town on the valley road that leads to Capilla in the province of Badajoz. As you cycle through the city, you should stop at the Gothic church of San Juan Bautista attached to the old Castillo de los Donceles with its beautiful round tower.

Capilla is a locality that is very close to the Serena Reservoir and within what is called the Sierra de Palanque, a small set of hills that go from the neighboring town of Peñalsordo to Capilla. It is a small town with few inhabitants.
Castle that is located right at the top of the town of Almadén and that is also called the Ermita Virgen del Castillo. The mountain has the same name as the castle.

The Almadén Mining Park is the living heritage of the two thousand years of Almadén exploitation. After the closure of the mining activity in 2003, the oldest mines in the world whose activity has been maintained to this day, show their secrets.
Mercury, the living silver of the Romans, is shown in the Park in all its aspects, the extraction of its mineral, cinnabar, mercury sulfide, its transformation in metallurgical furnaces, its physical and chemical properties, its uses and, of course, its eternal history.
The facilities of the Mining Park, the shafts, buildings and facilities are the heart of the Spanish assets registered on the list of World Heritage Sites under the name of Mercury Heritage. Almaden and Idrija.

World Heritage Site since 2012. Also called Plaza Nueva, whose construction was carried out between 1752 and 1754, it has a hexagonal shape and two stories high. It is made up of a single block, made up of twenty-four exterior homes, in whose interior courtyard the square itself was designed.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for race bikes. It features significant ascents and rolling hills, requiring a good level of fitness. While primarily on paved roads, be prepared for some challenging sections.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 80.9 km race bike tour is approximately 4 hours. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and time spent exploring the various points of interest along the way.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly on paved roads, making it suitable for race bikes. You will experience rolling hills and some more significant ascents, particularly around the Castillo de Chillón. The scenery includes cultural heritage sites, historic towns, and the unique industrial landscape of the Almadén mining region.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass several notable landmarks, including the Church of San Juan Bautista and Castle of the Donceles in Chillón, the Capilla and its Castle (likely the Virgen del Castillo chapel with panoramic views), and the historic Chillón Castle. The route also takes you through the Almadén Mining Park and past the unique Almadén Bullring. You'll also see the old stone bridge of Carlos IV and the Fuente de Dos Caños in Chillón.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences warm summers, so spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for cycling. The weather is milder, making the significant ascents more manageable and allowing for comfortable exploration of the historical sites.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the starting point in Chillón are not provided, towns like Chillón and Almadén typically offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability upon arrival, especially near the town centers or main attractions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Almadén Mining Park?
faqs.answer: Cycling the route itself does not require a permit. However, the route passes through the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, which may have specific regulations regarding activities. For the Almadén Mining Park, there may be entrance fees for specific attractions or guided tours within the park. It's recommended to check their official website for current admission details.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on public roads, dogs are generally allowed if kept under control and on a leash when off the bike, especially in towns or near historical sites. However, specific rules may apply within the Almadén Mining Park or other protected areas. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of official trails such as the Sendero del Peñón de Pez and the Camino de los Mineros - Etapa 1. You may also encounter parts of the Calle Casas Florido, Vereda de la Jornía, and Calle de la bajada al Pozuelo.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or refill water along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through towns like Chillón and Almadén, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refill water or grab a meal. Chillón, for example, has establishments like Restaurant La Peraleda. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially between towns.

faqs.question: What are some scenic viewpoints along the ride?
faqs.answer: The Capilla and its Castle (Virgen del Castillo chapel) offers stunning panoramic views across three autonomous communities. Further along, a small mountain pass leads into Extremadura province, revealing views of the Embalse de la Serena, Spain's largest reservoir. The roads between Almadén and Chillón also offer picturesque views of local 'parcelas'.

waypoints.name: Church of San Juan Bautista and Castle of the Donceles
